name: Chapch'ae
recipeIngredient: 1/2 Teaspoon Garlic powder or 1-2 cloves chopped garlic
1 Cup Cooked Spinach (parboiled), Rinsed and drained
Medium Green onions (scallions) -- Cut in 1-inch pieces
1 Package Shiitake mushrooms, Soaked in water
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the beef and pork into fine strips. Fry with soy sauce, sugar, black pepper, and sesame oil until well cooked and tender.
2. Parboil the spinach and rinse in cold water. Squeeze out excess water and chop into small strips.
3. Cook the noodles in plenty of boiling water until soft (2-4 minutes). Rinse in cold water. Cut noodles crosswise 2-3 times with scissors.
4. Fry carrots and onions gently together in a lightly greased pan (sesame seed oil or margarine) for 10 minutes to soften; do not brown.
5. Soak dried mushrooms in warm water for 10 minutes, then cut into strips and fry the same way as vegetables. Also sauté regular mushrooms.
6. Just before serving, saute the Chinese noodles in a large deep pan with 1 tablespoon soy sauce, 1 tablespoon sesame oil, 1/4 teaspoon salt, and 1 tablespoon sesame seeds.
7. (Optional) Beat the egg; fry in one sheet, fold into thirds, then cut into thin strips.
8. Combine all ingredients, including spinach: season to taste. The dish can be served warm or cold.
